name: Fanatec 15Nm
description: Wheel base and in-game settings for Fanatec DD bases running at 15Nm in Forza Motorsport. Based on community-recommended settings from Fanatec forums and sim racing guides.

wheelBase.name: Fanatec Direct Drive
wheelBase.notes: Applies to CSL DD (8Nm boost kit), GT DD Pro (8Nm boost kit), DD1, DD2, or ClubSport DD+ at 15Nm peak torque. Settings sourced from Fanatec community forums — no official Fanatec game-specific profile exists for Forza Motorsport (2024).

fanalab.title: Fanalab / Base Settings
fanalab.description: Configure in Fanalab or on the wheel base directly. These are community-recommended starting points.

settings.name: SEN
settings.description: Steering range — 900° matches Forza's default. Use Auto if your base supports it.

settings.name: FF
settings.description: Force feedback strength — reduced from 100 at 15Nm to avoid clipping. Increase for weaker bases.

settings.name: FFS
settings.description: Force feedback mode — Peak for maximum detail at lower overall force.

settings.name: NDP
settings.description: Natural damper — light damping to reduce oscillation on straights.

settings.name: NFR
settings.description: Natural friction — off for cleanest FFB signal.

settings.name: NIN
settings.description: Natural inertia — off to keep wheel responsive.

settings.name: INT
settings.description: Interpolation — off for most direct FFB. Increase to 3-4 if FFB feels grainy.

settings.name: FEI
settings.description: Force effect intensity — max for strong kerb and rumble strip effects.

settings.name: FOR
settings.description: Force — maximum base output, controlled by FF above.

settings.name: SPR
settings.description: Spring — off. Let the game handle centring.

settings.name: DPR
settings.description: Damper — off. Using NDP for smoother damping instead.

settings.name: BLI
settings.description: Brake force indicator — vibrate at 50% to feel lockup.

settings.name: SHO
settings.description: Vibration motor — full for gear shift and ABS feedback.

settings.name: MPS
settings.description: Multi-position switch — auto detect.

inGamePresets.name: Detailed & Responsive
inGamePresets.description: High road feel and pneumatic trail. Prioritises information over weight. Best for learning tracks and improving consistency.

settings.name: Vibration Scale
settings.description: Moderate vibration — enough to feel tire slip without overwhelming the wheel.

settings.name: Force Feedback Scale
settings.description: 50% at 15Nm avoids clipping. Start here and increase by 5 until you feel deadspots.

settings.name: Steering Sensitivity
settings.description: Linear — do not change. 1:1 steering input.

settings.name: Steering Linearity
settings.description: Linear — do not change. 1:1 mapping.

settings.name: Force Feedback Understeer
settings.description: Lightens wheel on understeer — helps feel front wash.

settings.name: Force Feedback Minimum Force
settings.description: Ensures FFB is always present, even at low speed.

settings.name: Wheel Damper Scale
settings.description: Light in-game damping — most comes from base NDP.

settings.name: Centre Spring Scale
settings.description: Off — artificial spring hides real FFB.

settings.name: Pneumatic Trail Scale
settings.description: Maximum tire feel through the wheel — single best slider for sensing grip.

settings.name: Mechanical Trail Scale
settings.description: Above default for stronger suspension and geometry feedback.

settings.name: Road Feel Scale
settings.description: Slightly above default for surface texture detail.

settings.name: Load Sensitivity
settings.description: Moderate — keeps wheel weight manageable in high-downforce cars.

settings.name: Dynamic Damper Behaviour
settings.description: Default — balanced damper response.

inGamePresets.name: Heavy & Realistic
inGamePresets.description: Higher forces and load sensitivity for a heavier, more physical experience. Closer to a real GT3 car's steering weight. Demanding on arms over long stints.

settings.name: Vibration Scale
settings.description: Strong vibration for pronounced tire slip and collision feedback.

settings.name: Force Feedback Scale
settings.description: Higher force — may clip in high-downforce corners. Monitor for dead spots.

settings.name: Steering Sensitivity
settings.description: Linear — do not change.

settings.name: Steering Linearity
settings.description: Linear — do not change.

settings.name: Force Feedback Understeer
settings.description: Less understeer lightening — heavier wheel at all times.

settings.name: Force Feedback Minimum Force
settings.description: Lower minimum — allows more dynamic range.

settings.name: Wheel Damper Scale
settings.description: Moderate damping for a heavier, planted feel.

settings.name: Centre Spring Scale
settings.description: Off — always off.

settings.name: Pneumatic Trail Scale
settings.description: Slightly reduced — avoids overloading feel when combined with high FFB Scale.

settings.name: Mechanical Trail Scale
settings.description: Default — balanced with higher overall force.

settings.name: Road Feel Scale
settings.description: Reduced slightly to avoid harshness at higher FFB levels.

settings.name: Load Sensitivity
settings.description: Full — wheel gets very heavy under load, realistic weight transfer.

settings.name: Dynamic Damper Behaviour
settings.description: Default — balanced damper response.

inGamePresets.name: Smooth Endurance
inGamePresets.description: Softer forces and damping for long races. Reduces arm fatigue while keeping enough information to drive consistently. Good for 30+ minute stints.

settings.name: Vibration Scale
settings.description: Lower vibration to reduce fatigue over long stints.

settings.name: Force Feedback Scale
settings.description: Light overall force — easy on the arms.

settings.name: Steering Sensitivity
settings.description: Linear — do not change.

settings.name: Steering Linearity
settings.description: Linear — do not change.

settings.name: Force Feedback Understeer
settings.description: More lightening on understeer — easier to hold through long corners.

settings.name: Force Feedback Minimum Force
settings.description: Moderate minimum to maintain feel at low speeds.

settings.name: Wheel Damper Scale
settings.description: Higher damping smooths out the wheel for comfort.

settings.name: Centre Spring Scale
settings.description: Off — always off.

settings.name: Pneumatic Trail Scale
settings.description: High but not maxed — still plenty of grip feel.

settings.name: Mechanical Trail Scale
settings.description: Default — no need to push with lower forces.

settings.name: Road Feel Scale
settings.description: Reduced to soften road bumps and kerb harshness.

settings.name: Load Sensitivity
settings.description: Reduced — prevents wheel getting too heavy under load.

settings.name: Dynamic Damper Behaviour
settings.description: Slightly more damping for smoother transitions.

tips: Always set Steering to Simulation in Driving Assists before touching FFB settings.
Start with the Detailed & Responsive preset and adjust from there.
Force Feedback Scale is the most important slider — if you feel dead spots or sudden loss of detail, it's clipping. Lower it by 5%.
Centre Spring Scale should always be 0 — it creates an artificial dead zone that fights real FFB.
Steering Sensitivity and Linearity should always stay at 50 — changing these breaks the 1:1 input mapping.
Pneumatic Trail is the single most important slider for feeling grip. Keep it high.
If the wheel oscillates on straights, increase NDP on the base by 5 at a time.
If FFB feels grainy or noisy, increase INT on the base to 3-4.
These are starting points — every driver prefers different weight and detail levels.
